objective: Make Vyre 0.4.2 and Weir 0.1.0 release-ready end to end
success_criteria: 1. CUDA-first execution path is documented, benchmarked, and selected as the fast release substrate.
2. WGPU fallback is functional, tested, benchmarked, and never hides a CUDA or CPU downgrade.
3. Megakernel is the default high-throughput runtime path where legal, including paired speculation evidence.
4. Non-megakernel dispatch is retained only with measured or architectural justification.
5. Optimization infrastructure proves at least 4096 concrete rewrite/pass opportunities across families.
6. Optimization passes have correctness tests and before/after benchmark evidence.
7. Weir alias, reaching-def, points-to, callgraph, slicing, summary, loop, and fixpoint facts integrate into Vyre optimization.
8. The distributed C parser parses a full selected Linux subsystem corpus with AST/semantic contract evidence.
9. At least 12 proof workload families compare CUDA against serious CPU baselines with reproducible artifacts; the current matrix carries 13 required rows.
10. At least ten formerly CPU-only workload families prove 100x+ wins or block release.
11. Conformance gates block release for every claimed op/backend path with zero blocked_release OP_MATRIX rows.
12. Test organization is modular, including distributed parser CLI evidence for tools/vyrec.
13. Documentation is coherent and every release claim links to concrete evidence.
14. Crate metadata, feature surfaces, readmes, examples, license, and version policy are consistent for Vyre 0.4.2 / Weir 0.1.0.
15. Final hygiene review finds no unbounded caches, hidden fallbacks, placeholders, library panics, unactionable errors, or undocumented public API.

prompt_to_artifact_checklist.explicit_requirement: Vyre manifests, dependency hints, lockfile path packages, docs, release notes, packaging, and product-scoped tags use the selected Vyre 0.6.1 / Weir 0.1.0 version story.
CUDA is the documented, benchmarked, optimized primary release substrate.
WGPU remains a real fallback path with conformance and benchmark evidence, not an untested branch.
Megakernel is the default high-throughput path where legal and has latency/condition workload evidence.
Optimization scale is proved by at least 4096 verified semantic-preserving generated cases.
Optimization families have before/after benchmark evidence and pass-family attribution.
DSE, store-to-load forwarding, LICM, loop fusion, and loop fission consume Weir alias facts and prove before/after impact.
Bounded e-graph or egglog-family saturation is present where it beats hand rewrites and has semantic plus benchmark evidence.
Weir exposes SSA, IFDS, reaching defs, points-to, alias, callgraph, slicing, summaries, loops, and fixpoint facts, and Vyre consumes those facts where they unlock safer optimization.
At least 12 proof workload families have reproducible CPU/GPU benchmark artifacts.
At least ten release workloads prove 100x+ CUDA wins against CPU-SOTA baselines.
The distributed C parser parses the selected full Linux subsystem corpus with AST, diagnostic, and throughput evidence.
required_artifacts_or_commands: cargo_full run --bin xtask -- c-parser-corpus --corpus DIR -I LINUX_INCLUDE_DIR -D CONFIG_SYMBOL=1 --output release/evidence/parser/c-parser-linux-subsystem.json

prompt_to_artifact_checklist.explicit_requirement: vyre-frontend-c, tools/vyrec, Weir, and Surge/SurgeC parser ownership boundaries are coherent and documented.
Conformance blocks release for every claimed op/backend pair.
Massive tests are split into fixtures, contracts, properties, backend tests, corpus tests, benchmarks, and regressions.
Normal, adversarial, property, conformance, corpus, benchmark, gap, and fuzz verification are present.
User, contributor, benchmark, conformance, parser, optimization, and release docs link to concrete evidence.
Every release crate has coherent metadata, features, docs, readme, license, and version policy.
No shipped stubs, hidden fallbacks, unbounded resources, hot-path sleeps, unactionable errors, or stale docs remain.

The final audit maps every explicit requirement, named artifact, generator command, and gate to evidence and refuses completion while any requirement is open, blocked, missing, empty, or semantically weak.

requirements.title: Manifest, tag, docs, and release-note version story is resolved
CUDA is the documented, tested, benchmarked fast release path
WGPU fallback is semantically correct, tested, and benchmarked
Megakernel is the default high-throughput runtime path where legal
Optimization corpus contains at least 4096 distinct semantic-preserving cases
Optimization families have before/after benchmark evidence
